name: BEN_EXTRACT_FILE_DETAILS
description: BEN_EXTRACT_FILE_DETAILS  Stores enrollment XML file data.

columns.Name: EXTRACT_FILE_DETAIL_ID
columns.Comments: Primary key

columns.Name: SOURCE_TYPE
columns.Comments: Determines from where the File came from

columns.Name: FILE_TYPE
columns.Comments: File type could be XML or text

columns.Name: INTERNAL_FILE_NAME
columns.Comments: System Given Name

columns.Name: SEQUENCE
columns.Comments: If the file is split, the sequence is maintained

columns.Name: FILE_FRAGMENT
columns.Comments: Actual File

columns.Name: CARRIER_ID
columns.Comments: Healt Care Provider Id

columns.Name: ENTERPRISE_ID
columns.Comments: Business Group

columns.Name: FILE_LOCATION
columns.Comments: Indicates location of  the file finally uploaded

columns.Name: REQUEST_ID
columns.Comments: Enterprise Service Scheduler: indicates the request ID of the job that created or last updated the row.

columns.Name: ESS_REQUEST_ID
columns.Comments: Schedular Request Id

columns.Name: PARENT_REQUEST_ID
columns.Comments: Stores Parent request Id in a case where the file is split

columns.Name: VERSION
columns.Comments: File Version when saved multiple time for the same ess request Id

columns.Name: CONTENT_ID
columns.Comments: Content Repository id

columns.Name: GROUP_ID
columns.Comments: If the File is split, the group Id will be the first Primary key Id of those files

columns.Name: SOURCE
columns.Comments: Indicates the file source, whether it is Java or plsql or manual

columns.Name: DOC_ID
columns.Comments: Store any extrnal Document Id

columns.Name: RUN_DATE
columns.Comments: Stores Date when the transmission was executed

columns.Name: STATUS
columns.Comments: Stores if the file is active , pending or inactive

columns.Name: ATTR_CHAR01
columns.Comments: For any future use

columns.Name: ATTR_CHAR02
columns.Comments: For any additional use in future

columns.Name: ATTR_CHAR03
columns.Comments: For any future use

columns.Name: ATTR_NUM01
columns.Comments: For any future use to store number

columns.Name: ATTR_NUM02
columns.Comments: For any future use to store number

columns.Name: ATTR_NUM03
columns.Comments: For any future use to store number

columns.Name: ATTR_DATE01
columns.Comments: Sore any future date data

columns.Name: ATTR_DATE02
columns.Comments: For any future use to store date

columns.Name: ATTR_DATE03
columns.Comments: For any future use to store date

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.
